message:
fix(ci): normalize CRLF to LF in artifact digest hashing for Windows parity

On Windows with git autocrlf=true, text files are checked out with CRLF
line endings while Unix uses LF. The snapshot and artifact digest functions
hash raw file bytes, causing identical content to produce different hashes
across platforms — breaking CI on Windows runners.

Strip \r bytes from file content before feeding into the BLAKE3 hasher in
both locations:
- src/conformance.rs: snapshot::digest_dir() used for conformance snapshots
- tests/ext_conformance_artifacts.rs: digest_artifact_dir() used for
  artifact integrity checks

This ensures the same logical content produces the same hash regardless
of the platform's line-ending convention, completing the Windows CI
portability fixes alongside the recent path separator normalization
(bb54f060, 7281aa39).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
